response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said brings genuine engineering breadth across languages and a strong craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions), and has touched high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic. However, the resume contains no evidence of the experimentation platform work that is the core of this role — no A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging, experiment assignment, rollout-risk controls, telemetry pipelines, or statistical analysis. Growth engineering is entirely absent from the career narrative. The most senior titles held are Staff-level, and the recent trajectory (direct-mail pricing tooling, small-consultancy AI agent work) does not demonstrate the cross-team, enterprise-scale technical leadership expected at Principal/IC6.
strengths: Broad language coverage across C, C++, C#, Java, and TypeScript/JavaScript maps cleanly onto the JD's required language list
High-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, caching) shows comfort with production scale

Consistent software-craftsmanship track record — TDD, trunk-based development, open-source testing libraries, and community teaching align with the mentorship and code-quality expectations of a Principal IC
concerns: Zero evidence of experimentation platform work: no A/B testing, feature flagging, experiment assignment, bias detection, or rollout-risk management — the primary deliverables of this role
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure work described anywhere in the resume; the JD's data-aptitude and measurement-platform requirements are entirely unaddressed
Career history concentrated in small-to-mid-size companies with no demonstrated cross-team technical leadership or enterprise-scale stakeholder alignment expected at Principal/IC6

key_factors.factor: No telemetry or metrics platform background
